text.title: idempotent-write-tools Failure Modes
text.summary: Four failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Generating the idempotency_key inside the tool execute function (`key = uuid4()`).
antipattern.reason: Every retry gets a fresh key; dedup never fires. The point of the key is stability across retries.

section.p: Symptom: duplicate side effects on transient retries. Root cause: in-tool key generation. Fix: require key as parameter from the agent; tool execute uses the supplied key, never generates one.

antipattern.reason: Bloats schema; the model has to fabricate values; tokens wasted for no benefit.

section.p: Symptom: read tool schemas include a key parameter that's always populated with junk. Root cause: misapplied rule. Fix: remove the parameter from read schemas; document the asymmetry.

antipattern.description: `*_preview` internally calls upstream dry-run but still mutates a cache or counter.
antipattern.reason: Any side effect on preview defeats the safety property; agents can no longer run preview speculatively.

section.p: Symptom: preview emits OTel events; counter values drift; cache state changes. Root cause: not actually pure. Fix: audit every preview path; remove cache writes and counters; if upstream dry-run mutates, gate behind an explicit no-op flag.

antipattern.description: Catalog exposes a single-call destructive operation (`delete_user(id)`, `deploy(service)`) without a preview/apply pair.
antipattern.reason: Agent has no chance to inspect; retry on timeout double-executes. Human review is impossible.

section.p: Symptom: production incident where retry caused double-deploy or double-charge. Root cause: missing preview/apply split. Fix: remove single-call form from catalog; expose `*_preview` and `*_apply` only; require `preview_hash` on apply.
